title: Tender Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: A comforting and flavorful Moroccan dish featuring spring onions and a glassy-jelly texture, perfect for a dinner gathering. The halal-friendly recipe uses lamb as the main protein source.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in the bottom of a clay or ceramic tagine or a Dutch oven over medium heat.
Add the lamb cubes and cook until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and grated ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Add the cumin, coriander, paprika, cinnamon, salt, and black pepper and cook for 1 minute.
Add the chopped spring onions and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the lamb shoulder back to the tagine and pour in the honey.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook, covered, for 1 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Stir in the chickpeas and cook for an additional 10 minutes.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped fresh parsley or cilantro.
Let the tagine rest for 10 minutes before serving to allow the flavors to meld and the sauce to thicken further.
Strain the sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a clean saucepan and discard the solids.
Simmer the sauce over low heat until it has thickened to a glassy-jelly consistency, about 10-15 minutes.
Taste and adjust the seasoning as needed before serving.
